evals.prompt: I am a software engineer who spent 6 years building internal tools for HVAC distributors. My brother runs a small HVAC service business and knows a lot of owners in the region. I want to make money with AI and eventually build a micro-SaaS, but I am open to starting with services. I do not have an audience. Help me figure out the best path.
evals.expected_output: A differentiated recommendation that favors a legal hybrid or service-to-software path over generic AI content plays, explains the HVAC edge clearly, and includes validation steps plus a build handoff.
assertions: Response includes a section header named Differentiation Profile
Response includes a section header named Top Opportunities
Response includes a section header named Best Bet
Response includes a section header named 7-Day Validation Plan
Response includes a section header named Build Spec
Response includes a section header named Kill Criteria
Response explicitly explains why this user can win in HVAC or why the user has an edge
Response recommends or seriously considers a hybrid ladder such as service to automation to product
Response includes a JSON block with a best_bet field

evals.prompt: I am a former compliance consultant for small financial firms. I have strong domain knowledge and a few warm contacts, but only 8 to 10 hours a week and almost no desire to become a content creator. I care about staying fully legal. I want something AI can accelerate. What opportunity should I pursue?
evals.expected_output: A legally grounded set of opportunities that uses compliance credibility as the main differentiator, avoids defaulting to pure content, and produces a ranked recommendation with validation and stop conditions.
assertions: Response includes a section header named Differentiation Profile
Response includes a section header named Top Opportunities
Response explicitly notes legal or compliance constraints
Response explicitly downgrades or avoids pure content or media as the main business
Response explains why this user can win using compliance expertise or warm contacts
Response includes kill criteria or stop conditions
Response includes a JSON block with an opportunities array

evals.prompt: I am a senior data analyst at an ecommerce SaaS company. I know a lot about merchant reporting pain, but I do not directly own budget and my network is mostly peers, not founders. I would love a micro-SaaS, but I am not sure if I should sell a service first. Give me your honest recommendation.
evals.expected_output: An honest recommendation that weighs the weak buyer access against strong workflow knowledge, states whether a service-first wedge is smarter than immediate software, and returns both a readable report and structured JSON.
assertions: Response includes a section header named Differentiation Profile
Response includes a section header named Best Bet
Response discusses the user's weak direct buyer access or distribution limits
Response explains whether a service first wedge should come before software
Response explains why this user can win using merchant reporting knowledge
Response includes a 7 day validation plan
Response includes a JSON block with a user_profile object
